hints.text: Coverage = only **5 cities**: Vientiane, Vang Vieng, Luang Prabang, Savannakhet, Pakse — all **Gen3**
bullets: Quick tells: Luang Prabang = history+hills+palms · Vang Vieng = distinct mountains · Vientiane = no roof rack, big city · Savannakhet = very flat · Pakse = red soil, arid, hills

hints.text: Everywhere except Vientiane: **silver car w/ 2-bar roof rack + black side mirrors** visible

shared_with.note: Guatemala has a similar silver car.
shared_with.diff: Spanish signage + Latin-American environment → Guatemala; Lao script → Laos.
Driving side is the fastest Laos-vs-Thailand decider.

shared_with.note: Thailand's yellow plates are only for commercial vehicles (taxis).
shared_with.diff: Yellow on ORDINARY cars → Laos; also check driving side.

shared_with.note: Cambodia uses white plates on private vehicles.

hints.text: **Lao script**: compact & curvy with diacritics above/below — like Thai but with **fewer straight lines**; stop signs have **2 characters** (Thai: 3), sometimes Latin 'STOP' below
Majority **Buddhist** — ornate temples w/ gold-leaf detail (Thailand/Cambodia similar)
**Striped curbs** in many colours (red/yellow/black + white) throughout; some lamp posts w/ red-white diagonal stripes that stop above the base
**Square concrete poles w/ pinhole rows top-to-bottom**

shared_with.note: Similar poles (pinholes only near the top there).

hints.text: Square concrete **black-white bollards with black band(s)**; red-white cuboid bollards a few metres off the road
Mekong riverside, low mountains/hills, palms; ex-royal capital & UNESCO site — historical dark-wood + French colonial architecture; **blue sign backs only here**
**Rugged karst mountain range** visible from almost everywhere (least coverage of the 5 cities; actual coverage lies south of the map label); very touristy (hotel/guesthouse signs)
Only area **without the roof-rack car**; much rural coverage SE — Thailand/Cambodia toss-ups: check driving side (TH left) & plates

**Road 450** east: made entirely of concrete blocks, one of the few divided highways; Nong Beuak divided highway = distinctive **street lamps** (also on road 13's divided part — different angle); conservation-area road: hilly + dry forest, good pavement w/ coloured curbs + dashed outer lines (only single-lane road with b&w / y&w curbs)
The only **trekker** in Laos: the monumental **Patuxai arch** war memorial (partly tripod coverage)
**Very flat** — only visible mountains are across the Mekong in Thailand; soil slightly red-tinged (less than Pakse); on the map hidden under the 'Mukdahan' label — look west of Xeno
Distinct **red soil**, uniquely dry vegetation + dry hills; the **1380 m Mekong bridge** is the only covered major bridge in Laos
